_meta.project: ContentSpy AI — SaaS Competitive Intelligence & Automated Content Platform
_meta.architect_notes: This is a complex multi-tenant SaaS with 5 major subsystems: (1) spy engine with 7 methods, (2) AI content generation with multi-provider token tracking, (3) multi-platform publishing including a custom WordPress plugin, (4) social media distribution, (5) credit/billing engine. The build order is strictly: infrastructure → auth → credits → spy → content → publishing → plugin → social → analytics. The WordPress plugin is a parallel deliverable that must be treated as a standalone product. Token cost tracking is non-negotiable and must be wired into every AI call from day one.
main_risks: Credit race conditions under concurrent jobs — must use Redis atomic DECR with floor check from P4 onward
Multi-tenant data isolation — ALL queries must scope by workspace_id, missing scope = data leak
Playwright at scale — memory leaks, proxy rotation, anti-bot detection — needs circuit breaker
Midjourney has no official API — Discord bot automation brittle — must have DALL-E/SD fallback wired from start
TikTok Content Posting API — requires approved app, file pre-upload, strict codec (H.264) — plan for 2-week API review delay
Long article generation (>3000 words) must be fully async chunked — never synchronous
OpenRouter pricing is volatile — never hardcode model costs, always fetch live from /models endpoint

stack.wordpress_plugin: Pure PHP, no composer deps, custom REST endpoints

rules_for_ai: ALWAYS check depends_on before starting any task
ALWAYS update task status when done
NEVER skip a task without marking skipped with a reason in notes
NEVER modify existing code without checking all linked tasks
ALWAYS respect the file structure defined in PROJECT_CONTEXT.md
ALWAYS verify every acceptance_criteria before marking a task done
If a task fails, mark it blocked with the reason in technical_notes
NEVER merge two responsibilities into one task
NEVER start a new phase if the previous phase has blocked tasks
NEVER hardcode API keys, model prices, or platform limits — use config
ALWAYS scope database queries by workspace_id — no exceptions
ALWAYS wrap credit operations in atomic transactions or Redis DECR
ALWAYS record token usage (model, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, cost_usd) for every AI call

phases.name: Project Initialization & Infrastructure
phases.description: Laravel project setup, Docker environment, base configuration, all third-party service connections verified

tasks.title: Initialize Laravel 11 project with Docker environment
tasks.description: Create the Laravel 11 project and define the full Docker Compose stack including PHP, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis, Meilisearch, Soketi. This is the foundation everything runs on.
acceptance_criteria: docker-compose up starts all services without error
php artisan serve responds on configured port
MySQL 8 container is reachable
Redis container is reachable (redis-cli ping returns PONG)
MongoDB container is reachable
Meilisearch container is reachable on port 7700
tasks.technical_notes: Use Docker Compose profiles: core (PHP, MySQL, Redis, Nginx) and full (adds MongoDB, Meilisearch, Soketi, Playwright, FFmpeg microservices). Playwright and FFmpeg run as separate Node.js containers.

tasks.title: Configure environment variables and service connections
tasks.description: Set up all .env variables for every external service. Verify each connection works before proceeding. This task must not be marked done until every service ping succeeds.
acceptance_criteria: php artisan db:show returns MySQL connection success
Redis PING returns PONG via Laravel Cache
MongoDB connection test returns OK
Meilisearch health endpoint returns 200
R2 bucket connection verified via Storage::put test file
Stripe API key validated via stripe.com ping
Resend API key validated
tasks.technical_notes: Create a php artisan contentspy:verify-connections command that pings all services and outputs a connection report. Run this command at end of task.

tasks.title: Install and configure all Laravel packages
tasks.description: Install Sanctum, Horizon, Telescope, MongoDB driver, Meilisearch Scout driver, Stripe SDK, Sentry, Resend, and all other required packages.
acceptance_criteria: php artisan horizon:publish succeeds
Telescope dashboard loads at /telescope
Scout configured with Meilisearch driver
MongoDB Eloquent model can be instantiated without error
Stripe SDK instantiates without error
tasks.technical_notes: Pin package versions in composer.json to avoid breaking changes. Laravel 11 includes Sanctum by default via install:api.

tasks.title: Create global configuration files
tasks.description: Create all custom config files: contentspy.php (platform settings), ai-models.php (model pricing table), credits.php (credit costs per action), platforms.php (social platform rate limits).
acceptance_criteria: config('ai-models.pricing.gpt-4o.input') returns 5.00
config('credits.actions.article_generation_per_1000_words') returns 5
config('platforms.instagram.max_posts_per_day') returns 25
config('contentspy.auto_spy_min_credits') returns 50
tasks.technical_notes: ai-models.php must include ALL models: gpt-4o, gpt-4-turbo, gpt-3.5-turbo, claude-3-5-sonnet, claude-3-opus, dall-e-3, stable-diffusion-xl. OpenRouter pricing fetched live — store in Redis cache with 1h TTL, not in config file.

tasks.title: Initialize Playwright Node.js microservice
tasks.description: Build the Playwright scraping microservice as a standalone Express.js app running in Docker. It exposes HTTP endpoints called by Laravel for all headless browser tasks.
acceptance_criteria: POST /scrape returns HTML content for a given URL
Service handles timeout gracefully (returns error JSON, not crash)
Service is reachable from Laravel container via PLAYWRIGHT_SERVICE_URL
Playwright chromium launches without errors in Docker
Health endpoint GET /health returns {status: ok}
tasks.technical_notes: Use Playwright with chromium. Implement circuit breaker: if 5 consecutive requests fail, service returns 503 for 60s. User-agent rotation pool must have minimum 20 agents. Proxy support via PROXY_LIST env variable.

tasks.title: Initialize FFmpeg Node.js microservice
tasks.description: Build the FFmpeg video assembly microservice as a standalone Express.js app. Receives image URLs + audio URL, assembles MP4 video for TikTok/Instagram Reels.
acceptance_criteria: POST /assemble accepts {images: [], audio_url, duration, format} and returns MP4 file path
Outputs H.264 video at 1080x1920 (TikTok portrait) by default
Service handles missing audio gracefully (silent video fallback)
GET /health returns {status: ok}
Assembled video uploaded to R2 and URL returned in response
tasks.technical_notes: FFmpeg must be installed in Dockerfile. Output formats: mp4 (H.264/AAC). TikTok requires: H.264, 720p-1080p, 15-600s, AAC audio. Instagram Reels: H.264, 1080x1920, 3-90s. Implement resource limits: max 2 concurrent assemblies per container.

phases.name: Database — Migrations
phases.description: All MySQL and MongoDB schema definitions. Every table created in strict dependency order. No models until all migrations are done.

tasks.title: Migrate users table with workspace and plan fields
tasks.description: Modify the default Laravel users migration to add: plan, role, is_active, google_id, avatar, timezone, locale.
acceptance_criteria: php artisan migrate runs without error
users table contains all new columns
google_id has unique index
tasks.technical_notes: Do NOT create a new migration — modify the existing one. Run php artisan migrate:fresh after modification.

tasks.title: Create workspaces migration
tasks.description: Workspaces are the multi-tenant unit. Every resource belongs to a workspace. One user can own multiple workspaces (agency use case).
acceptance_criteria: Migration runs without error
credits_reserved column exists for atomic credit reservation
ulid generated automatically on insert
tasks.technical_notes: Use ULID not UUID for workspace ID — shorter and sortable. credits_reserved tracks in-flight credit reservations to prevent overdraft.

tasks.title: Create workspace_users pivot migration
tasks.description: Many-to-many between users and workspaces with role. A user can belong to multiple workspaces with different roles.
acceptance_criteria: Migration runs without error
Composite unique key on workspace_id+user_id prevents duplicate membership
tasks.technical_notes: accepted_at NULL means invitation pending. invited_by tracks who sent the invite for audit.

tasks.title: Create credit_transactions migration
tasks.description: Immutable ledger of every credit debit and credit. This is the source of truth for credit balance — balance is always derived from this table.
acceptance_criteria: Migration runs without error
amount allows negative values (debits)
balance_after captured at transaction time for instant balance lookup
tasks.technical_notes: NEVER update or delete rows in this table — it is an append-only ledger. balance_after makes balance queries O(1) without summing all transactions.

tasks.title: Create token_usage_logs migration
tasks.description: Every AI API call records tokens consumed and cost in USD. This table is the foundation for margin analysis and billing transparency.
acceptance_criteria: Migration runs without error
cost_usd stored as DECIMAL(10,6) for micro-cost precision
All indexes created
tasks.technical_notes: This table grows fast — partition by month in production. Use created_at for time-range queries. admin dashboard aggregates from this table.

tasks.title: Create sites migration
tasks.description: WordPress sites managed by the user. Each site is the central entity that connects competitors, content, publishing, and social accounts.
acceptance_criteria: Migration runs without error
wp_app_password and plugin_secret columns marked for encryption in model
plugin_api_key unique index created
tasks.technical_notes: wp_app_password and plugin_secret stored encrypted via AES-256 using APP_KEY. Never log these values. plugin_api_key is the token the WP plugin uses to authenticate with SaaS.

tasks.title: Create competitors migration
tasks.description: Competitors tracked per site. One competitor can be monitored via multiple spy methods simultaneously.
acceptance_criteria: Migration runs without error
active_methods is JSON array of method strings
Three confidence thresholds exist for suggest/generate/publish
tasks.technical_notes: active_methods stores array like ['rss', 'sitemap', 'html_scraping']. confidence_threshold_publish should default to 85 to prevent accidental full autopilot.

tasks.title: Create spy_detections migration
tasks.description: Every article detected from every competitor via any spy method. Deduplication happens here via content_hash.
acceptance_criteria: Migration runs without error
content_hash UNIQUE prevents duplicate detections across methods
All status transitions trackable via status field
tasks.technical_notes: content_hash = SHA-256 of normalized URL + title. Normalization: lowercase, strip UTM params, strip trailing slash. This is the dedup key across all 7 spy methods.

tasks.title: Create content_suggestions migration
tasks.description: Content opportunity cards generated from spy detections. Each card contains the AI-generated brief for article creation.
acceptance_criteria: Migration runs without error
expires_at defaults to 30 days from creation (set in model)
article_id nullable FK for when article is generated from suggestion
tasks.technical_notes: expires_at set in model boot method: +30 days from created_at. Expired suggestions auto-archived via scheduled command.

tasks.title: Create articles migration
tasks.description: Generated articles with all content, SEO metadata, generation metadata, and publishing status.
acceptance_criteria: Migration runs without error
total_cost_usd tracked per article for margin analysis
generation_status tracks each step of 8-step pipeline
tasks.technical_notes: content stored as LONGTEXT to handle 5000+ word articles. inline_images JSON array of {url, r2_key, alt_text, position} objects. outline JSON stores H2/H3 structure before generation.

tasks.title: Create social_accounts and social_posts migrations
tasks.description: Social media accounts connected per site, and posts published or scheduled per article per platform.
acceptance_criteria: Both migrations run without error
access_token and refresh_token marked encrypted in model
social_posts.retry_count supports up to 3 retry attempts
tasks.technical_notes: Facebook and Instagram share the same OAuth app but have separate social_accounts rows. token_expires_at used by token refresh service to proactively refresh before expiry.

tasks.title: Create workflow_logs and spy_job_logs migrations
tasks.description: Audit logs for workflow execution per site and for every spy scan job. These are the debug and compliance records.
acceptance_criteria: Both migrations run without error
workflow_logs captures every step of DETECT→SCORE→SUGGEST→GENERATE→PUBLISH→DISTRIBUTE
spy_job_logs records new_detections count per run
tasks.technical_notes: These tables are append-only. Purge records older than 90 days via scheduled command to control storage growth.

tasks.title: Create notifications and webhook_endpoints migrations
tasks.description: In-app notifications store and user-configured webhook endpoints for event delivery.
acceptance_criteria: Both migrations run without error
webhook_endpoints.events stores array of event strings
failure_count auto-disables webhook after 10 consecutive failures (enforced in service)
tasks.technical_notes: Webhook secret used for HMAC signature on outgoing payloads. failure_count incremented on each failed delivery, reset on success.

phases.name: Database — Models & Relations
phases.description: All Eloquent models with relations, casts, scopes, and encrypted attributes. MongoDB models for spy snapshots.

tasks.title: Create User and Workspace models with relations
tasks.description: User belongs to many Workspaces. Workspace has one owner (User). Setup all fillable, casts, hidden, and relations.
acceptance_criteria: $user->workspaces returns collection of Workspace
$workspace->owner returns User
$workspace->members returns all workspace users with pivot role
User model has currentWorkspace() method returning active workspace from session
tasks.technical_notes: Add HasCurrentWorkspace trait to User. Workspace must have global scope or helper to always scope queries by current workspace.

tasks.title: Create Site, Competitor, and SpyDetection models
tasks.description: Core spy engine models. Site has many Competitors. Competitor has many SpyDetections. SpyDetection deduplicates via content_hash.
acceptance_criteria: $site->competitors returns collection
$competitor->detections returns ordered by created_at DESC
SpyDetection::findOrCreateByHash() creates or returns existing by content_hash
Competitor model has scopeWithAutoSpy() local scope
tasks.technical_notes: All models must use WorkspaceScopedModel trait that auto-applies workspace_id scope. Encrypted attributes: site->wp_app_password, site->plugin_secret.

tasks.title: Create Article, ContentSuggestion models with pipeline state machine
tasks.description: Article model manages the 8-step generation pipeline state. ContentSuggestion manages the opportunity card lifecycle.
acceptance_criteria: $article->advancePipelineStep() transitions generation_status to next step
$article->tokenUsageLogs() returns all related logs
$article->totalCostUsd attribute computed from token_usage_logs
ContentSuggestion auto-sets expires_at to +30 days in creating event
ContentSuggestion::scopePending() filters status=pending and not expired
tasks.technical_notes: Use state machine pattern for generation_status transitions. Invalid transitions should throw InvalidStateTransitionException. This prevents jobs from jumping steps.

tasks.title: Create CreditTransaction, TokenUsageLog models
tasks.description: Financial models. CreditTransaction is append-only. TokenUsageLog records every AI API call. Both provide aggregate methods for dashboards.
acceptance_criteria: CreditTransaction::boot() prevents update and delete (throws exception)
TokenUsageLog::scopeByModel() filters by model string
TokenUsageLog::totalCostByWorkspace() returns sum grouped by provider
Workspace->creditBalance attribute returns last balance_after from transactions
tasks.technical_notes: CreditTransaction is immutable — override save(), update(), delete() to throw LogicException if row already exists. balance_after field makes balance O(1) lookup.

tasks.title: Create SocialAccount, SocialPost models
tasks.description: Social media integration models. SocialAccount stores encrypted OAuth tokens with refresh logic. SocialPost tracks per-platform publishing state.
acceptance_criteria: SocialAccount->access_token cast to encrypted
SocialAccount->isTokenExpired() returns bool
SocialAccount->refreshToken() calls platform-specific refresh and updates DB
SocialPost->scopePending() returns posts ready to publish
SocialPost->canRetry() returns false when retry_count >= 3
tasks.technical_notes: Each platform has different token expiry: Facebook 60 days, Instagram 60 days, TikTok 30 days, Pinterest never expires (until revoked). refreshToken() must handle each case.

tasks.title: Create MongoDB models for spy snapshots
tasks.description: MongoDB Eloquent models for raw spy data: RssSnapshot, SitemapSnapshot, HtmlSnapshot. These store raw data before processing.
acceptance_criteria: RssSnapshot::latestForCompetitor() returns most recent snapshot
SitemapSnapshot::diffUrls() returns new URLs vs previous snapshot
Models use MongoDB connection defined in database.php
tasks.technical_notes: MongoDB used only for raw spy data — all processed data goes to MySQL. MongoDB documents TTL: 30 days (set via TTL index on created_at). Use mongodb/laravel-mongodb package.

phases.name: Authentication, Authorization & Security
phases.description: Full auth stack: registration, login, Google OAuth, workspace switching, role-based authorization, API key management.

tasks.title: Implement registration, login, logout API endpoints
tasks.description: Standard email/password auth via Sanctum SPA. Registration creates user + default workspace in one transaction.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/auth/register creates user + workspace in single transaction
POST /api/auth/login returns Sanctum token
POST /api/auth/logout revokes current token
Registration grants Starter plan with 500 free credits
Invalid credentials return 401 with clear message
Rate limit: 5 login attempts per minute per IP
tasks.technical_notes: Registration must be atomic: user + workspace + credit_transaction (500 credits grant) + workspace_users (owner role) in one DB transaction. If any step fails, all rolled back.

tasks.title: Implement Google OAuth login
tasks.description: Google social login via Laravel Socialite. Creates account if new user, links to existing if email matches.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/auth/google redirects to Google OAuth consent screen
GET /api/auth/google/callback handles response, creates/links user
New Google user gets workspace + 500 credits same as registration
Existing user linked by email match — google_id updated
Returns Sanctum token on success
tasks.technical_notes: Store google_id for future logins. Avatar URL saved to users.avatar. Use Socialite stateless() for SPA compatibility.

tasks.title: Implement workspace authorization (roles and permissions)
tasks.description: Define all permissions per role. Implement Laravel Gates and Policies for every protected resource.
acceptance_criteria: Owner and Admin can: create/delete sites, manage billing, invite users
Editor can: manage competitors, approve articles, publish
Writer can: create articles, view suggestions
Viewer can: only read dashboard data
Unauthorized action returns 403
All policies tested with unit tests
tasks.technical_notes: Use PHP 8.1 Enum for WorkspaceRole. PermissionService::can(user, action, workspace) is the single entry point for all permission checks — never inline role checks.

tasks.title: Implement credit reservation and atomic deduction service
tasks.description: CreditService handles all credit operations. Reservation (lock credits before job starts), confirm (deduct on success), refund (release on failure). Race-condition safe.
acceptance_criteria: CreditService::reserve(workspace, action, amount) uses Redis SETNX lock + DB update credits_reserved
CreditService::confirm(reservationId) deducts from balance, releases reservation, logs transaction
CreditService::refund(reservationId) releases reservation without deduction
Two concurrent reserve() calls for same workspace cannot overdraft
InsufficientCreditsException thrown if balance - reserved < amount
Every operation creates CreditTransaction record
tasks.technical_notes: Use Redis atomic operations: MULTI/EXEC or Lua script to check-and-decrement in one atomic step. DB credits_reserved column tracks in-flight reservations. NEVER subtract credits without a reservation ID.

tasks.title: Implement plan limit enforcement middleware
tasks.description: Middleware that enforces plan limits: max sites, max competitors, max posts per day, max social platforms. Checked at job creation time.
acceptance_criteria: Starter plan: blocked at 4th site creation attempt
Starter plan: blocked at 6th competitor creation attempt per site
Pro plan: blocked at 16th site creation attempt
Full autopilot blocked on Starter plan with 402 response
White-label blocked on non-Agency plan with 402 response
Clear error messages indicating which limit was hit and upgrade path
tasks.technical_notes: Plan limits defined in config/contentspy.php, never hardcoded in middleware. Returns 402 Payment Required for limit violations with upgrade_url in response body.

phases.name: AI Provider Integration & Token Tracking
phases.description: All AI provider clients with token tracking wired in. Every AI call goes through the AiGateway which records usage automatically.

tasks.title: Build AiGateway — central AI dispatch service
tasks.description: Single entry point for all AI text generation. Handles provider selection, fallback chain, token recording, and cost calculation. No direct provider calls outside this service.
acceptance_criteria: AiGateway::generate(prompt, model, context) returns AiResponse with text + token counts
AiResponse contains: text, model, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, cost_usd
Token usage automatically logged to token_usage_logs after every call
Fallback: if primary model fails → try fallback model → log both attempts
TokenPricingService::calculate(model, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens) returns cost_usd
OpenRouter pricing fetched from /models endpoint, cached in Redis for 1 hour
All providers implement AiProviderInterface
tasks.technical_notes: AiGateway must be the ONLY class that calls external AI APIs. All other services call AiGateway. This ensures 100% token tracking coverage. TokenPricingService uses config/ai-models.php for known models, live fetch for OpenRouter models.

tasks.title: Build ImageGenerationGateway with provider fallback
tasks.description: Central image generation service. Handles DALL-E 3, Stable Diffusion (Replicate), and Midjourney (Discord bot). All image costs logged.
acceptance_criteria: ImageGenerationGateway::generate(prompt, size, provider) returns {url, r2_key, cost_usd}
Generated image uploaded to R2, public URL returned
Midjourney provider wraps Discord bot automation, returns image URL
Fallback: Midjourney fails → DALL-E 3 → Stable Diffusion
Cost logged to token_usage_logs with images_count=1 and cost_usd
MidjourneyProvider requires MIDJOURNEY_DISCORD_TOKEN env var — throws ConfigException if missing
tasks.technical_notes: Midjourney via Discord is fragile. Implement with 60s timeout and retry once. If it fails twice, mark provider as temporarily unavailable (Redis flag, 30min TTL) and use fallback immediately.

tasks.title: Build TTS and video script generation services
tasks.description: Text-to-speech via ElevenLabs/OpenAI TTS. Video script generation from article content. Both feed into FFmpeg microservice for TikTok/Reels.
acceptance_criteria: TtsService::generate(text, voice) returns {audio_url, duration_seconds, cost_usd}
Audio file uploaded to R2, URL returned
VideoScriptService::fromArticle(article) extracts 3-5 key points as video script
Cost logged to token_usage_logs with video_seconds and cost_usd
Fallback: ElevenLabs fails → OpenAI TTS
tasks.technical_notes: ElevenLabs pricing: $0.30 per 1000 characters. OpenAI TTS: $0.015 per 1000 characters. Log chars_count and cost_usd for both.

phases.name: Spy Engine — All 7 Methods
phases.description: All 7 competitor spy methods as independent services. Each callable independently and via the orchestrator. All consume credits and log to spy_job_logs.

tasks.title: Build SpyOrchestrator and base SpyMethod contract
tasks.description: SpyOrchestrator coordinates which methods to run for a competitor. All spy methods implement SpyMethodInterface. Deduplication via content_hash enforced here.
acceptance_criteria: SpyOrchestrator::run(competitor) dispatches all active_methods as async jobs
SpyMethodInterface::detect(competitor) returns array of DetectedArticle DTOs
SpyDeduplicator::process(detections) filters duplicates via content_hash before DB insert
OpportunityScorer::score(detection) returns 0-100 score
Credits reserved before each method run, confirmed after, refunded on failure
spy_job_logs row created for every run
tasks.technical_notes: OpportunityScorer factors: article length (longer = higher), keyword density, freshness (newer = higher), competitor domain authority. Weights configurable in config/contentspy.php.

tasks.title: Implement RSS feed spy method
tasks.description: Parse RSS/Atom feeds, compare with last snapshot in MongoDB, extract new articles, score them.
acceptance_criteria: Parses RSS 2.0 and Atom 1.0 feeds
Compares with RssSnapshot in MongoDB — only new articles returned
Saves new snapshot to MongoDB after processing
Handles malformed XML without crashing (returns empty array + logs error)
Respects configurable interval — won't re-scan before interval elapsed
1 credit consumed per run
tasks.technical_notes: Use SimplePie or custom XML parser. Normalize feed item: guid → source_url, pubDate → published_at. content_hash = SHA-256(normalized_url + title).

tasks.title: Implement HTML scraping spy method via Playwright microservice
tasks.description: Call Playwright microservice to scrape competitor blog index, extract article links, compare with previous HTML snapshot.
acceptance_criteria: Sends POST request to Playwright service with competitor blog URL
Extracts all article links from response HTML
Compares with HtmlSnapshot in MongoDB — only new URLs returned
Handles Playwright service timeout (30s) gracefully
Handles anti-bot detection response — marks competitor as temporarily unscrapable
3 credits consumed per run
tasks.technical_notes: Circuit breaker: if Playwright returns 503 or times out 3 times in a row for a competitor, set Redis flag to pause HTML scraping for that competitor for 1 hour.

tasks.title: Implement sitemap, Google News, and social signal spy methods
tasks.description: Three additional spy methods: sitemap XML parsing, Google News RSS monitoring, and social signal tracking via platform APIs.
acceptance_criteria: SitemapSpyMethod: parses sitemap-index recursively, returns new URLs vs snapshot
GoogleNewsSpyMethod: queries Google News RSS for competitor domain, returns new articles
SocialSignalSpyMethod: fetches Twitter/X API v2 recent tweets for competitor handle
All three handle API errors gracefully and log to spy_job_logs
Credits consumed: sitemap=1, google_news=2, social_signal=2
tasks.technical_notes: Twitter API v2 requires Bearer token. Store in config. Google News is via RSS: https://news.google.com/rss/search?q=site:domain.com — no API key needed.

tasks.title: Implement keyword gap and SERP monitoring spy methods
tasks.description: Two SEO-focused spy methods using third-party APIs: SEMrush/Ahrefs for keyword gaps, SERP API for rank tracking.
acceptance_criteria: KeywordGapSpyMethod: calls SEMrush API with user's API key, returns keyword gap opportunities
SerpMonitoringSpyMethod: calls SERP API for tracked keywords, detects ranking changes
Both methods require user-provided API keys — throw MissingApiKeyException if absent
5 credits consumed per keyword gap run, 3 per SERP check
tasks.technical_notes: User API keys stored encrypted in site.settings JSON field. Fetch via $site->settings['semrush_api_key']. Never log or expose these keys.

tasks.title: Build auto-spy scheduler and queue jobs
tasks.description: Laravel scheduled commands that trigger auto-spy for all competitors with auto_spy=true on their configured intervals. Dispatches jobs to Horizon queues.
acceptance_criteria: DispatchAutoSpyCommand runs every 15 minutes via scheduler
Only dispatches for competitors where next_scan_due <= now()
Skips competitors belonging to workspaces with < 50 credits
Jobs sent to dedicated 'spy' queue in Horizon
Job failure: marks competitor last_scanned_at, logs error, does NOT retry infinitely
Max 3 retries with exponential backoff
tasks.technical_notes: Use Horizon queue priorities: spy=high, content_generation=default, publishing=low. This ensures spy jobs don't starve when generation queue is full.

phases.name: Content Generation Pipeline
phases.description: 8-step async content generation pipeline. Each step is a separate job. Article model tracks state. All AI calls go through AiGateway.

tasks.title: Build ContentPipelineOrchestrator and step jobs
tasks.description: Orchestrates the 8-step pipeline. Each step dispatches the next step on success. Failed step marks article as failed and refunds credits.
acceptance_criteria: ContentPipelineOrchestrator::start(suggestion, options) creates Article and dispatches GenerateOutlineJob
Each job advances article.generation_status on success
Each job dispatches next job in chain on success
Any job failure: article.generation_status = failed, credits refunded, user notified
Long articles (>2000 words) generated section-by-section in GenerateArticleBodyJob
Total tokens and cost_usd accumulated across all steps on article record
tasks.technical_notes: Chain: outline → body (chunked) → SEO → duplicate_check → images → format → ready. Each job is idempotent — safe to retry. Use article.generation_status to detect if step already completed (skip if so).

tasks.title: Implement outline generation step
tasks.description: Generate H2/H3 article structure from suggestion brief. Uses AiGateway. Stores outline JSON on article before body generation.
acceptance_criteria: Generates outline with 4-8 H2 sections + 2-3 H3 per H2 for articles >1500 words
Outline stored as JSON on article.outline
Prompt includes: title, keywords, tone, word_count_target, competitor_angle
Token usage logged via AiGateway automatically
3 credits consumed
tasks.technical_notes: Prompt must instruct model to return JSON only: {sections: [{h2, h3s: [], word_count_target}]}. Parse JSON from response — if parsing fails, retry once with stricter prompt.

tasks.title: Implement chunked article body generation
tasks.description: Generate article body section by section. Each H2 section is a separate AiGateway call to avoid timeouts on long articles.
acceptance_criteria: Generates each H2 section independently via separate AiGateway calls
Includes previous section summary in prompt for context continuity
Assembles all sections into final article.content
word_count updated on article after generation
All token usage per section accumulated on article.total_tokens_used
5 credits per 1000 words consumed
tasks.technical_notes: Section prompt includes: section title, target word count, previous section summary (last 200 words), article tone, focus keyword. This maintains coherence across chunks.

tasks.title: Implement SEO optimization, duplicate check, and formatting steps
tasks.description: Three final content steps: SEO meta generation, duplicate content check via Meilisearch, and WordPress Gutenberg block formatting.
acceptance_criteria: SeoOptimizerService generates: meta_title (<=60 chars), meta_description (<=160 chars), slug, focus_keyword
DuplicateCheckerService searches Meilisearch index for similar published articles
Duplicate score > 85% blocks publishing, sets article.duplicate_check_passed = false
WordPressFormatterService converts markdown/HTML to Gutenberg blocks JSON
Supports both Gutenberg blocks and Classic editor HTML output (per site config)
2 credits consumed for SEO step, 1 for duplicate check
tasks.technical_notes: Meilisearch duplicate check: index all published articles by site. Search by article title + first paragraph. Similarity score = Meilisearch ranking score * 100.

tasks.title: Build content suggestion generation from spy detections
tasks.description: Service that turns SpyDetection records into ContentSuggestion cards. Called after every spy run that finds new detections.
acceptance_criteria: SuggestionGeneratorService::fromDetection(detection) creates ContentSuggestion
Uses AiGateway to generate: suggested_title, content_angle, target_keywords, recommended_word_count
Opportunity score from SpyDetection copied to ContentSuggestion
Duplicate suggestion check: same site + same focus keyword within 7 days → skip
2 credits consumed per suggestion generated
Token usage logged
tasks.technical_notes: Prompt: 'Given this competitor article: {title}, {excerpt}. Suggest a better angle for {site_niche}. Return JSON: {title, angle, keywords[], word_count, tone}'. JSON-only response enforced.

phases.name: WordPress Publishing — REST API & Plugin Bridge
phases.description: WordPress publishing via REST API direct connection and via the ContentSpy Connect plugin. Plugin is a standalone PHP deliverable.

tasks.title: Build WordPressPublisher service
tasks.description: Publishes articles to WordPress via REST API or plugin endpoint. Handles both connection types transparently.
acceptance_criteria: WordPressPublisher::publish(article, site) auto-selects client based on site.connection_type
Creates WP post with: title, content, excerpt, status, categories, tags, slug, meta SEO fields
Uploads featured image to WP media library, sets as featured image
Sets Yoast SEO fields: _yoast_wpseo_title, _yoast_wpseo_metadesc, _yoast_wpseo_focuskw
Sets Rank Math fields: rank_math_title, rank_math_description, rank_math_focus_keyword
Updates article: wp_post_id, wp_post_url, wp_published_at, publish_status
1 credit consumed per publish
tasks.technical_notes: WordPressRestClient uses Application Password in Authorization header. WordPressPluginClient uses HMAC-signed requests to /wp-json/contentspy/v1/publish. Both implement WordPressClientInterface.

tasks.title: Build ContentSpy Connect WordPress plugin — core
tasks.description: The WordPress plugin that connects a WP installation to ContentSpy SaaS. Core: settings page, API key verification, HMAC authentication.
acceptance_criteria: Plugin activates without PHP errors on WordPress 5.8+ with PHP 8.0+
Settings page appears under ContentSpy → Settings in WP Admin
User pastes SaaS API key → plugin calls SaaS /api/plugin/verify → shows Connected/Error status
HMAC verification: plugin validates X-ContentSpy-Signature header on all requests
HMAC uses SHA-256 HMAC of request body with shared secret, compared via hash_equals()
All plugin options stored in wp_options with prefix contentspy_
Plugin creates custom DB table contentspy_logs on activation
tasks.technical_notes: HMAC comparison MUST use hash_equals() not strcmp() to prevent timing attacks. Plugin secret = SHA-256 random key generated on first activation, stored in wp_options encrypted. No composer — pure PHP.

tasks.title: Build WordPress plugin — custom REST endpoints
tasks.description: All /wp-json/contentspy/v1/ endpoints: publish, status, posts, sync, delete. Each endpoint HMAC-verified.
acceptance_criteria: POST /wp-json/contentspy/v1/publish creates WP post with all fields, returns {post_id, post_url}
GET /wp-json/contentspy/v1/status returns site health JSON
GET /wp-json/contentspy/v1/posts returns paginated post list
POST /wp-json/contentspy/v1/sync triggers full post sync back to SaaS
DELETE /wp-json/contentspy/v1/posts/{id} moves post to trash
All endpoints reject requests without valid HMAC signature (401)
Rate limit: 60 requests/minute per endpoint (stored in transients)
tasks.technical_notes: Publisher class handles: post creation, category/tag lookup-or-create, media upload from URL (file_get_contents + wp_upload_bits), Yoast and RankMath meta via update_post_meta().

tasks.title: Build WordPress plugin — admin widget and auto-update
tasks.description: WP admin dashboard widget showing pending ContentSpy articles. Plugin auto-update mechanism via SaaS API.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard widget shows pending article count and list with approve/reject buttons
Admin bar badge shows pending count
Plugin checks for updates via GET /api/plugin/version on admin_init
Update available notice shown with one-click update
SaaS serves plugin zip from R2 storage
Shortcode [contentspy_suggestions] renders pending suggestions widget
tasks.technical_notes: Auto-update uses WordPress plugin update hooks: pre_set_site_transient_update_plugins and plugins_api. Plugin zip served from R2 with signed URL (15min expiry).

tasks.title: Build plugin distribution and SaaS plugin management endpoints
tasks.description: SaaS-side endpoints for plugin: verify connection, serve plugin zip, handle sync webhooks from plugin. Plugin download from SaaS dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/plugin/download returns signed R2 URL for plugin zip
POST /api/plugin/verify validates API key, returns {site_id, secret, connected: true}
GET /api/plugin/version returns {current_version, download_url, changelog}
POST /api/plugin/sync receives post list from WP, syncs to SaaS articles
Plugin API key generation: 32-char random hex, stored hashed in sites.plugin_api_key
Plugin zip built from wordpress-plugin/ directory and uploaded to R2 on deploy
tasks.technical_notes: Plugin API key stored as SHA-256 hash in DB, compared with hash_equals() on verify. Raw key shown to user ONCE at connection time. Build script: zip wordpress-plugin/ → upload to R2 as contentspy-connect-v{version}.zip.

tasks.title: Build publishing queue with rate limiting and retry logic
tasks.description: Publishing queue enforces max posts per day per site. Failed publishes retry 3 times with exponential backoff.
acceptance_criteria: PublishingQueueService::queue(article) checks daily post count before queuing
Rejects queueing if site has reached max_posts_per_day limit
PublishToWordPressJob retries 3 times with 5min, 15min, 45min delays
After 3 failures: publish_status = failed, user notified, credits refunded
Daily post count tracked in Redis (key: publish_count:{site_id}:{date}, TTL 25h)
tasks.technical_notes: Use Redis for daily counter — atomic INCR. Key expires after 25h to auto-reset at midnight. Do not use DB for rate limit tracking — too slow.

phases.name: Social Media Publishing
phases.description: OAuth connections, content adapters, and publishing for Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, Pinterest. Video pipeline for Reels/TikTok.

tasks.title: Build OAuth connection flows for all 4 social platforms
tasks.description: OAuth2 flow for Facebook/Instagram (Graph API), TikTok (Content API), Pinterest (API v5). Stores and refreshes tokens.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/social/{platform}/connect redirects to platform OAuth
GET /api/social/{platform}/callback stores tokens in social_accounts
Facebook flow returns Page list — user selects which page to connect
Instagram connected via linked Facebook Business account
TokenRefreshService::refreshAll() refreshes all near-expiry tokens (< 7 days remaining)
TokenRefreshService runs daily via scheduler
tasks.technical_notes: Facebook and Instagram share one OAuth app (same Graph API credentials). TikTok requires Content Posting API scope — app must be approved by TikTok (plan for approval delay). Pinterest OAuth: HTTPS callback required.

tasks.title: Build content adapters for all 4 platforms
tasks.description: Each platform adapter receives an Article and generates platform-native content: caption, hashtags, image specs, video specs.
acceptance_criteria: All adapters implement SocialAdapterInterface::adapt(article) → SocialPostDTO
Facebook: generates 300-500 char post + image via ImageGenerationGateway
Instagram: generates caption (<=2200 chars) + hashtags + 1080x1080 image
TikTok: generates video script (3-5 points) + calls TtsService + queues FFmpeg assembly
Pinterest: generates vertical 1000x1500 image + title (<=100 chars) + description (<=500 chars)
All image/video costs logged to token_usage_logs
tasks.technical_notes: SocialPostDTO contains: platform, post_type, caption, hashtags, media_urls, video_url, scheduled_for. Adapters must NOT call publishing APIs — only prepare content. Publishing is separate job.

tasks.title: Build social publishing jobs for all 4 platforms
tasks.description: Platform-specific publishing jobs that call Graph API, TikTok API, Pinterest API. Retry logic, rate limit enforcement, credit refund on failure.
acceptance_criteria: Facebook: POST to Graph API /page/feed or /page/photos with correct payload
Instagram: two-step publish (create container → publish container)
TikTok: pre-upload video file → publish via Content Posting API
Pinterest: POST to /pins with image + title + description + link
All jobs: 3 retries with exponential backoff (5min, 15min, 45min)
After 3 failures: social_post.status = failed, credits refunded, webhook fired
Platform daily limits enforced via Redis counters before each publish
tasks.technical_notes: TikTok video upload is a two-step process: init upload → upload chunks → publish. Video must be H.264, max 4GB, 15-600s. Always validate video specs before uploading.

tasks.title: Build social media analytics fetcher
tasks.description: Fetch post performance metrics from all 4 platforms and store in social_posts.metrics JSON.
acceptance_criteria: Fetches metrics for all published posts older than 24h
Facebook: likes, comments, shares, reach, impressions
Instagram: likes, comments, saves, reach, plays (reels)
TikTok: views, likes, comments, shares
Pinterest: impressions, saves, clicks, outbound_clicks
Metrics stored in social_posts.metrics JSON, metrics_updated_at updated
Job runs daily via scheduler
tasks.technical_notes: Metrics are best-effort — if platform API fails, log error and continue. Never fail article workflow due to metrics fetch error.

phases.name: API Layer — Controllers, Routes, Resources
phases.description: All REST API endpoints. Every controller is thin — delegates to services. All responses via API Resources. Rate limiting per plan.

tasks.title: Build workspace and site management API
tasks.description: CRUD endpoints for workspaces and sites. Site connection verification endpoint.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/workspaces returns user's workspaces
POST /api/workspaces creates workspace (plan limits enforced)
CRUD /api/sites for site management
POST /api/sites/{id}/verify-connection tests WP connection, returns status
POST /api/sites/{id}/switch sets current workspace in session
All responses use API Resources (no raw model exposure)
tasks.technical_notes: SiteController::verifyConnection() calls WordPress REST API or plugin endpoint and returns detailed status including WP version, plugin version, available categories.

tasks.title: Build competitor and spy management API
tasks.description: CRUD for competitors. Trigger manual spy scans. View spy detections. Configure spy methods and auto-spy settings.
acceptance_criteria: CRUD /api/competitors with site scoping
POST /api/competitors/{id}/scan triggers immediate spy scan for all active methods
GET /api/competitors/{id}/detections returns paginated detections
PATCH /api/competitors/{id}/auto-spy toggles auto_spy and updates thresholds
Plan limits enforced on competitor creation
tasks.technical_notes: Manual scan dispatches RunCompetitorSpyJob immediately (not scheduled). Credits reserved at API time, not job execution time.

tasks.title: Build content suggestions and article management API
tasks.description: Endpoints for suggestion management and article CRUD. Trigger generation. Approve/reject workflow.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/suggestions with filters: site_id, status, min_score
POST /api/suggestions/{id}/accept triggers GenerateSuggestionJob
POST /api/suggestions/{id}/reject with reason
POST /api/suggestions/bulk-action for bulk accept/reject/schedule
GET /api/articles with generation_status filter
POST /api/articles/{id}/approve sets review_status=approved, queues publish
POST /api/articles/{id}/publish immediately publishes to WordPress
GET /api/articles/{id}/token-usage returns token consumption breakdown
tasks.technical_notes: Article token-usage endpoint aggregates token_usage_logs for that article: grouped by model, total cost_usd, credits_consumed. Useful for user transparency.

tasks.title: Build credits, billing, and Stripe webhook API
tasks.description: Credit balance, transaction history, Stripe subscription management, credit pack purchases, Stripe webhook handler.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/billing/balance returns {balance, reserved, available, plan}
GET /api/billing/transactions returns paginated credit transaction history
GET /api/billing/token-usage returns token consumption report with cost_usd breakdown by model
POST /api/billing/subscribe creates Stripe subscription
POST /api/billing/buy-credits creates one-time Stripe payment intent
POST /webhooks/stripe handles: invoice.paid (grant credits), customer.subscription.deleted (downgrade plan)
Stripe webhook verified via Stripe-Signature header
tasks.technical_notes: invoice.paid webhook grants monthly credits atomically via CreditService. Never grant credits based on frontend confirmation — only from Stripe webhook. Idempotency: check Stripe event ID before processing.

tasks.title: Build notifications, webhooks, and settings API
tasks.description: In-app notification endpoints, user webhook configuration, user preferences and workspace settings.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/notifications returns unread notifications
POST /api/notifications/mark-read marks notifications as read
CRUD /api/webhook-endpoints
POST /api/webhook-endpoints/{id}/test sends test payload to webhook URL
PATCH /api/settings/workspace updates workspace settings
PATCH /api/settings/site/{id}/workflow updates workflow configuration
tasks.technical_notes: Webhook test fires a test event with example payload. Webhook delivery via SendWebhookJob — async, not inline. Secret returned only on creation, never again.

phases.name: Workflow Engine & Automation
phases.description: Per-site configurable workflow. Conditional logic, templates, audit logging. The brain that connects spy → suggest → generate → publish → distribute.

tasks.title: Build WorkflowEngine with step handlers and conditional logic
tasks.description: The workflow engine evaluates conditions and decides which steps to execute automatically vs which to pause for human review.
acceptance_criteria: WorkflowEngine::run(site, trigger, detection) executes configured workflow
WorkflowConditionEvaluator evaluates: opportunity_score thresholds, keyword_difficulty, plan limits
Full Autopilot: detect → suggest → generate → publish → distribute (all auto)
Human in Loop: detect → suggest (auto) → generate (auto) → PAUSE for review → publish (manual)
Spy Only: detect → suggest (auto) → STOP
Every step logged to workflow_logs with input/output/credits_consumed
Full autopilot blocked on Starter plan
tasks.technical_notes: WorkflowEngine is event-driven: each completed step fires a WorkflowStepCompleted event. StepHandlers listen and decide next action. This decouples steps and makes testing easier.

tasks.title: Build notification dispatch system (in-app, email, Slack, webhooks)
tasks.description: Unified notification system. Every important event fires the correct notification to the correct channels.
acceptance_criteria: All notifications fire via Laravel Notification system
In-app: stored in notifications table, pushed via Soketi/Echo
Email: sent via Resend driver with HTML templates
Slack: POST to user-configured webhook URL
Outgoing webhook: SendWebhookJob fires signed payload to all matching endpoints
LowCreditsNotification fires when workspace credits drop below user-configured threshold
User can configure per-notification-type: enabled channels (in-app, email, slack)
tasks.technical_notes: Use Laravel Notification channels: database, mail, broadcast (for real-time). Custom webhook channel implemented as Laravel Notification Channel.

phases.name: Frontend — React Application
phases.description: Full React 18 SPA with all dashboard views, spy management, content pipeline, publishing calendar, and analytics.

tasks.title: Initialize React app with routing, auth, and global state
tasks.description: Vite + React 18 + TailwindCSS + shadcn/ui setup. Auth flow, workspace switching, protected routes, TanStack Query global config.
acceptance_criteria: Vite dev server starts without errors
Protected routes redirect to /login if not authenticated
Workspace switching updates all data without page reload
TanStack Query configured with staleTime, retry, and error handling
apiClient auto-attaches Authorization header to all requests
apiClient intercepts 401 and triggers logout
tasks.technical_notes: Use Zustand for auth/workspace state. TanStack Query for all server state. apiClient wraps fetch with base URL, auth header, JSON parsing, and error normalization.

tasks.title: Build global dashboard and competitor activity feed
tasks.description: Main dashboard with site overview cards, real-time competitor activity feed, credit balance widget, pending suggestions count.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard loads all site cards with connection status indicators
Activity feed shows real-time competitor detections via WebSocket (Soketi)
Credit widget shows balance, reserved, available with progress bar
Low credit alert shown when below threshold
Pending suggestions count shown per site
tasks.technical_notes: Real-time activity feed via Laravel Echo + Soketi. Subscribe to workspace.{id} channel. Broadcast SpyDetectionEvent when new detection arrives.

tasks.title: Build spy management UI
tasks.description: Per-site competitor management, spy method configuration, detection list with opportunity scores, auto-spy settings.
acceptance_criteria: Add competitor with URL → system auto-detects RSS and sitemap URLs
Toggle each spy method on/off per competitor
Configure auto-spy interval and confidence thresholds with sliders
Detection list with score badges, method tags, and action buttons
Manual scan button triggers immediate spy run with loading state
Credit cost shown before confirming manual scan
tasks.technical_notes: Auto-detect RSS: try {domain}/feed, {domain}/rss, {domain}/rss.xml. Auto-detect sitemap: check robots.txt for Sitemap: directive. Show detected URLs to user for confirmation.

tasks.title: Build content pipeline UI — suggestions and article editor
tasks.description: Kanban-style content pipeline, suggestion cards, article generation progress, article review editor.
acceptance_criteria: Kanban columns: Suggested → Generating → Review → Scheduled → Published
Suggestion card shows: title, score badge, competitor source, estimated traffic, keywords
Accept suggestion → shows AI model selector + credit cost estimate → confirm → generation starts
Generation progress shows current step with animated indicator
Article editor: rich text editor for review, SEO fields, featured image preview
Token usage breakdown shows: model used, tokens consumed, cost in USD and credits
tasks.technical_notes: Generation progress updates via WebSocket. Subscribe to article.{id} channel. Server broadcasts ArticlePipelineStepCompleted event after each step.

tasks.title: Build publishing calendar and social media management UI
tasks.description: FullCalendar publishing calendar, social account connection flow, social post preview per platform.
acceptance_criteria: FullCalendar shows all scheduled WP posts and social posts color-coded by platform
Drag-and-drop rescheduling updates scheduled_for via API
Social accounts page shows connected accounts with token expiry warning
OAuth connect button opens popup window for platform auth
Social post preview shows platform-native card (FB, IG, TikTok, Pinterest mockup)
tasks.technical_notes: OAuth popup: window.open() → postMessage() back to parent on callback. Prevents full page navigation during OAuth flow.

tasks.title: Build analytics, billing, and admin dashboards
tasks.description: Analytics dashboard with Recharts, billing/credit management UI, and admin panel for SaaS owner.
acceptance_criteria: Credit consumption chart: daily bars, last 30 days, colored by action type
Token cost breakdown: pie chart by AI model, table with cost_usd per model
Billing page: current plan, usage vs limit, transaction history, upgrade buttons
Credit pack purchase flow: select pack → Stripe checkout → redirect back → balance updated
Admin dashboard (is_admin users only): MRR, total users, API cost vs credits sold, margin %
Admin token leaderboard: top 10 users by API cost this month
tasks.technical_notes: Admin dashboard accessible only to users with is_admin=true. Separate admin API routes with AdminMiddleware. Never expose other users' data in regular API.

phases.name: Background Jobs, Queues & Scheduling
phases.description: All Horizon queue configuration, scheduled commands, cleanup jobs, and monitoring setup.

tasks.title: Configure Laravel Horizon with queue priorities and worker pools
tasks.description: Horizon configuration with multiple worker pools for different queue priorities. Spy jobs high priority, generation default, social low.
acceptance_criteria: spy queue: 5 workers, high priority
content queue: 3 workers, default priority, 300s timeout (long generation)
publishing queue: 2 workers, low priority
social queue: 2 workers, low priority
notifications queue: 1 worker
Horizon dashboard accessible at /horizon (admin only in production)
Failed jobs visible in Horizon with retry button
tasks.technical_notes: content queue timeout must be 300s minimum to handle chunked article generation. Default Laravel timeout is 60s — will kill mid-generation jobs.

tasks.title: Implement all scheduled commands
tasks.description: All cron-scheduled commands: auto-spy dispatch, token refresh, suggestion expiry, data cleanup, metrics fetch, credit expiry.
acceptance_criteria: DispatchAutoSpyCommand: every 15 minutes
RefreshSocialTokensCommand: daily at 03:00
ExpireSuggestionsCommand: daily — archives suggestions older than 30 days
FetchSocialMetricsCommand: daily at 06:00
PurgeExpiredDataCommand: weekly — deletes workflow_logs > 90 days, MongoDB snapshots > 30 days
ExpireCreditsCommand: monthly — expires one-time pack credits after 90 days
SendLowCreditAlertsCommand: every 6h — checks all workspaces, fires notification if below threshold
tasks.technical_notes: PurgeExpiredDataCommand deletes MongoDB documents via TTL index (set on created_at field) — not via command. Command only handles MySQL cleanup.

phases.name: Testing & QA
phases.description: Unit tests, feature tests, integration tests. Every critical path covered. Focus on: credit integrity, auth, spy dedup, token tracking.

tasks.title: Write unit tests for CreditService and TokenPricingService
tasks.description: Critical financial logic must have 100% test coverage. Race condition simulation tests required.
acceptance_criteria: CreditService: test reserve → confirm flow
CreditService: test reserve → refund flow
CreditService: test InsufficientCreditsException when balance < amount
CreditService: test concurrent reservations cannot overdraft (run 10 parallel reservations)
TokenPricingService: test cost calculation for all known models
TokenPricingService: test OpenRouter live pricing fallback
All tests pass: php artisan test --filter CreditServiceTest
tasks.technical_notes: Concurrent reservation test: use parallel HTTP requests or Laravel's concurrency testing helpers. Assert final balance = initial - (successful reservations only).

tasks.title: Write feature tests for auth, site management, and spy engine
tasks.description: End-to-end feature tests for registration flow, workspace isolation, site connection, and spy detection deduplication.
acceptance_criteria: Registration: user + workspace + 500 credits created atomically
WorkspaceIsolation: user A cannot access user B's sites/articles/competitors
SpyDedup: same article detected by RSS + HTML scraping = one SpyDetection (not two)
AutoSpy: pauses when credits < 50, resumes when credits >= 50
All tests use RefreshDatabase trait
tasks.technical_notes: Workspace isolation tests are security tests. Run them with two separate authenticated users. Any 200 response to cross-workspace resource is a test failure.

tasks.title: Write feature tests for content pipeline and publishing
tasks.description: Test full article generation pipeline state machine. Test WordPress publishing via mocked WP API. Test credit consumption per pipeline step.
acceptance_criteria: Pipeline: article advances through all 8 states correctly
Pipeline: failed step sets article.generation_status = failed and refunds credits
Pipeline: total_tokens_used and total_cost_usd accumulated correctly across steps
WordPress: mock WP API returns success → article.wp_post_id set, publish_status = published
WordPress: mock WP API returns 401 → retry, then failed, credits refunded
Credits: each action type consumes exactly the configured credit amount
tasks.technical_notes: Mock external APIs using Http::fake() for WordPress REST API and AI providers. Never call real APIs in tests.

tasks.title: Write WordPress plugin unit tests
tasks.description: PHPUnit tests for the WordPress plugin: HMAC verification, endpoint responses, publisher logic.
acceptance_criteria: HMAC: valid signature accepted
HMAC: invalid signature rejected with 401
HMAC: timing attack safe (hash_equals used, not ==)
Publisher: post created with all fields correctly
Publisher: media uploaded from URL and set as featured image
Endpoints: all return correct HTTP status codes
tasks.technical_notes: Use WP_UnitTestCase from wordpress-develop test suite. Mock wp_remote_get/post for external HTTP calls. Run with: ./vendor/bin/phpunit (using WP test bootstrap).

phases.name: Performance, Security & Production Readiness
phases.description: Final hardening: query optimization, Redis caching, security audit, rate limiting, monitoring, and deployment configuration.

tasks.title: Security audit and hardening
tasks.description: Review all endpoints for: missing workspace_id scoping, encrypted field exposure, HMAC implementation, rate limits, input validation.
acceptance_criteria: All controllers verified: every query scoped by workspace_id
No encrypted fields (API keys, tokens, passwords) returned in any API response
All endpoints have rate limiting (throttle middleware)
Plugin HMAC uses hash_equals() confirmed in code review
Stripe webhook uses Stripe-Signature verification confirmed
No raw SQL queries (all via Eloquent with parameter binding)
SECURITY_AUDIT.md documents findings and fixes
tasks.technical_notes: Run php artisan route:list and manually verify each route has: auth middleware, workspace scope, rate limit. Create a checklist in SECURITY_AUDIT.md.

tasks.title: Performance optimization and caching layer
tasks.description: Add Redis caching for: OpenRouter model pricing, workspace credit balance, site status, suggestion counts. Optimize N+1 queries.
acceptance_criteria: OpenRouter /models response cached in Redis with 1h TTL
Workspace credit balance cached with 5min TTL, invalidated on transaction
Dashboard queries use eager loading (no N+1)
token_usage_logs queries use date range index
php artisan telescope:clear run to verify no N+1 in feature flows
All list endpoints paginated (never return unbounded collections)
tasks.technical_notes: Use Laravel Cache::remember() for all expensive reads. Cache key convention: {entity}:{id}:{attribute}. Always tag caches for bulk invalidation: Cache::tags(['workspace:123'])->flush().

tasks.title: Production deployment configuration
tasks.description: Docker production build, environment configuration, Forge/Coolify setup, Sentry error tracking, monitoring.
acceptance_criteria: docker-compose.prod.yml with production-optimized services
php artisan optimize runs in deploy script
Sentry DSN configured — errors captured in production
Horizon Supervisor configured and auto-restarts
Soketi deployed and WebSocket connections verified
R2 CORS configured for frontend image access
SSL configured on all domains
Scheduler (cron) configured for php artisan schedule:run every minute
tasks.technical_notes: Deploy script: git pull → composer install --no-dev → php artisan migrate --force → php artisan optimize → php artisan horizon:terminate (Supervisor restarts it). Zero-downtime if using Forge atomic deployments.
